data.title: Fiverr Stock Hits 52-Week Low - Here's Why
data.description:
While earnings topped estimates and margins expanded, slowing revenue growth, a double-digit drop in active buyers, and soft forward guidance rattled investors, sending the stock down more than 13%.

The company’s quarterly sales growth of 3.4% year over year to $107.17 million missed the analyst consensus estimate of $109.05 million.

Fiverr reported adjusted earnings per share of 86 cents, beating the analyst consensus estimate of 72 cents.

Key Metrics

Marketplace revenue slipped to $71.5 million in the fourth quarter of 2025, down 2.7% from $73.5 million a year earlier.

The platform counted 3.1 million annual active buyers as of December 31, 2025, a 13.6% drop from 3.6 million the prior year.

Despite fewer buyers, annual spend per buyer rose 13.3% to $342, compared with $302 a year ago.

The marketplace take rate for the twelve-month period ended December 31, 2025, edged down to 27.7%, up from 27.6% a year earlier.

Meanwhile, services revenue climbed 18.2% year over year to $35.6 million in the fourth quarter of 2025, up from $30.2 million in the year-ago quarter.

Margin Expansion and Cash Position

The adjusted gross margin rose 70 basis points to 84.7%, while the adjusted EBITDA margin expanded 470 basis points to 24.7%.

The operating income for the quarter was $5.90 million, compared to a loss of $(5.88) million last year.

As of December 31, 2025, Fiverr generated $21.87 million in operating cash flow and held $446.18 million in cash and equivalents.

Micha Kaufman, the company’s founder and CEO, emphasized that the company is experiencing a major shift in AI adoption, with the marketplace evolving to make human talent more essential.

Fiverr aims to remain the bridge between businesses and top human talent by embracing an agentic economy, where AI assists in managing complexity.

Kaufman shared his confidence in the company’s multi-year plan to lead this transition.

Ofer Katz, Fiverr’s CFO, highlighted the company’s record adjusted EBITDA margin as a sign of strong business health and progress in pivoting upmarket.

Outlook

Fiverr expects first-quarter fiscal 2026 revenue of $100.00 million-$108.00 million, against the analyst consensus estimate of $112.21 million.

It expects an adjusted EBITDA of $19 million-$23 million.

Fiverr now expects fiscal 2026 revenue of $380.00 million-$420.00 million versus the analyst consensus estimate of $457.70 million.

It expects an adjusted EBITDA of $60 million-$80 million.

announced executive leadership changes aimed at supporting long-term growth and increasing operational complexity.

Leadership Changes

The company announced executive leadership changes aimed at supporting long-term growth and increasing operational complexity.

Ofer Katz will remain President but will step down from the CFO role to focus on strategic investments and mergers and acquisitions.

Esti Levy Dadon has been promoted to CFO. A nearly decade-long veteran at Fiverr, she previously served as EVP of Finance and will now also oversee additional business and operational responsibilities.

The company also created a new Chief Business Officer position, appointing Jinjin Qian to the role. She will lead revenue, talent, fulfillment, and business operations after seven years heading Investor Relations and Strategy.

FVRR Price Action: Fiverr shares were down 13.13% at $11.38 during premarket trading on Wednesday. The stock is trading at a new 52-week low, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Redwire Stock On Wednesday?
data.description:
Shares of Redwire Corporation (NYSE:RDW) are climbing in Wednesday's premarket session as investors look toward the company’s upcoming fourth-quarter earnings report and broader market tailwinds.

The early move for Redwire coincides with a positive start for the wider markets. As of Wednesday morning, Nasdaq futures gained 0.57% while S&P 500 futures rose 0.52%. Sector-specific strength is also providing a lift, with the energy sector leading morning gains at 0.84%, followed by a 0.50% uptick in financials.

Technical Analysis

Over the past year, Redwire has faced significant challenges, with its stock price declining by 66.07%. Currently, the stock is trading 22.1% below its 20-day SMA of $10.59 and just 2% below its 100-day SMA of $8.42.

This positioning suggests a bearish short-term trend but a potential stabilization in the medium term. The stock’s 52-week range has seen a low of $4.87 and a high of $26.27, with today’s price of $8.26 placing it closer to the lower end of this spectrum.

The technical indicators present a mixed picture. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) stands at 41.01, indicating a neutral momentum, while the Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) is below the signal line, suggesting bearish pressure.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

The countdown is on: Redwire Corporation is set to report earnings on Feb. 25.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 16 cents (Up from $-1.38 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $99.47 million (Up from $69.56 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $10.93. Recent analyst moves include:

  • HC Wainwright & Co.: Buy (Maintains Target to $22.00) (Dec. 23, 2025)
  • Keybanc: Initiated with Sector Weight (Dec. 19, 2025)
  • Cantor Fitzgerald: Overweight (Lowers Target to $9.00) (Dec. 10, 2025)

RDW Price Action: Redwire shares were up 3.25% at $8.26 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Tactile Systems Stock Smashes 52-Week High After Strong Quarter
data.description:
This move is seen as a strategic expansion as the company seeks to bolster its market position in the medical technology sector, which is currently experiencing positive momentum.

The Acquisition

The acquisition announced on Tuesday involves an upfront cash payment of $6.8 million, with potential additional consideration based on future milestones.

The acquisition is expected to enhance Tactile’s capabilities in providing accurate and efficient disease identification and treatment options for lymphedema patients.

Lymphedema is chronic swelling, usually in arms or legs, caused by a damaged or blocked lymphatic system, often resulting from cancer treatment (node removal/radiation) or, less commonly, inherited, congenital issues.

LymphaTech’s technology includes a handheld device that delivers precise measurements of fluid volume and circumference.

Earnings Snapshot

Tactile Systems reported fourth-quarter earnings of 46 cents, better than the consensus of 44 cents.

Sales jumped 21% to $103.59 million, beating the Wall Street estimates of $93.80 million, attributable to lymphedema and airway clearance product lines.

Gross profit increased 26% to $81 million, operating income jumped from $12.5 million to $18.8 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $22.9 million compared to $16.2 million a year ago.

2026 Outlook

Tactile Systems forecasts fiscal 2026 sales between $357 million-$365 million compared to the consensus of $347.94 million.

The company expects adjusted EBITDA in the range of $49 million to $51 million.

Tactile’s stock is moving positively, reflecting its specific news while the overall market trends upward, indicating a favorable environment for growth-oriented stocks.

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 21.5% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 19.8% above its 100-day SMA, demonstrating strong short-term momentum. Shares have increased 57.05% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 48.93, which is considered neutral territory, indicating no immediate overbought or oversold conditions. Meanwhile, MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $36.00
  • Key Support: $32.00

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $31.38. Recent analyst moves include:

  • BTIG: Buy (Raises Target to $38.00) (Feb. 18)
  • Piper Sandler: Upgraded to Overweight (Raises Target to $35.00) (Jan. 5)
  • Lake Street: Buy (Raises Target to $32.00) (Nov. 4, 2025)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Tactile Systems Technology, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 64.05 — The stock is trading at a reasonable valuation relative to peers.
  • Quality: 80.91 — Indicates a strong balance sheet and operational efficiency.
  • Momentum: 94.33 — The stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Tactile Systems Technology’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score, indicating robust performance in the current market environment. However, the value score suggests that while the stock is performing well, it may be trading at a premium compared to some peers.

data.title: US Produces More Copper Than It Needs, Beating China On Self-Reliance— But One Critical Bottleneck Could Derail It
data.description:
Benchmark Mineral Intelligence has said that the U.S. can cater to 146% of its annual copper demand from domestic and overseas mines and scrap. This is a significant contrast to China, the world’s largest consumer, which can only meet 40% of its demand.

Benchmark’s analyst Albert Mackenzie pointed out that the U.S. produces more copper than it consumes, making it more self-reliant than China in terms of raw materials, reported the Financial Times on Wednesday.

The analyst told FT that while people often speak of China's strong self-sufficiency, the country is far from it, given how much it still needs.

However, despite having a robust domestic copper mining sector and meaningful scrap output, the U.S. exports large volumes of copper because of limited domestic processing capacity. Much of this material is refined into copper cathode overseas — mainly in China, and then shipped back to the U.S. for use by semi-fabricated product manufacturers.

Mackenzie emphasized that the bottleneck for the US lies in the limited processing capacity to convert raw copper into copper cathode, a key component used by manufacturers. He stated that there’s no point in producing more raw materials if they are not going to be processed domestically.

US Copper Stocks Surge Amid Tariff Fears

The U.S. has been quietly stockpiling copper, with 590,000 short tons sitting in COMEX-approved storage, the highest level in over 30 years. U.S. copper inventories have surged nearly 300% over the past year, surpassing the combined stocks of the LME and Shanghai exchanges.

The spike is driven by traders rushing shipments into the U.S. to get ahead of potential 15%–25% tariffs on refined copper, creating a domestic glut while global supplies tighten.

Meanwhile, President Donald Trump also unveiled a public-private $12 billion critical mineral stockpile to counter China’s dominance and increase self-reliance.

This stockpiling coincides with a surge in copper prices, which reached an all-time high of $14,268 a metric ton on the London Metal Exchange in January 2026, surging nearly 40% from October.

Copper Seen As Next Big Commodity Rally

Meanwhile, Chamath Palihapitiya named copper the top trade of 2026, highlighting that AI data centers could each require 50,000 tons, while new mine production would take over 20 years to scale.

However, Goldman Sachs warned that the large U.S. stockpile may appear significant, but it masks a structurally tight market. The bank warned that once tariff uncertainty eases, likely by mid-2026, much of the metal could exit inventories, potentially triggering a short-term price drop.

Disclaimer: This content was partially produced with the help of AI tools and was reviewed and published by Benzinga editors.

Image via Shutterstock
